documents.title: Daily Options Market Brief: S&P 500 Index
documents.content:
# Daily Options Market Brief: S&P 500 Index

**Date:** 2026-03-28  
**Domain:** Options Intelligence  
**Reference:** Underlying: S&P 500 Index (SPX), Regime: Post-FOMC Positive Gamma, KeyLevel: SPX Put Wall 5700

## Summary

SPX opened at 5820.50 (prev close 5795.30) with dealers positioned long gamma following the FOMC rate decision. Net GEX at the 5800 strike is +720.4, anchoring price action within a tight intraday range. The Post-FOMC Positive Gamma regime (gex 245.6) is expected to dampen realized volatility through the session.

## Details

Put wall support at 5700 (strength 85.2) provides downside cushion. The prior Negative Gamma Regime flipped through Transition and Positive Gamma before settling Post-FOMC. Watch for mean reversion toward the 5800 magnet strike on any deviation.

## Recommendations

Favor positive-gamma strategies on SPX: 0DTE Gamma Scalp and Put Wall Bounce are aligned with the current regime. Reduce directional size; harvest theta into the 5800 pin.

---

*Document generated for Options Intelligence context graph application.*

**Date:** 2026-03-28  
**Domain:** Options Intelligence  
**Reference:** Underlying: SPDR S&P 500 ETF (SPY), Regime: Post-FOMC Positive Gamma, KeyLevel: SPY Call Wall 585

## Summary

SPY trades at 582.05, up from 579.53, tracking the S&P 500 index in Post-FOMC Positive Gamma conditions. The 580 strike shows GEX of +280.1 with dealers long gamma. Call wall resistance at 585 (strength 72.8) is the key upside level to monitor.

## Details

SPY 580C Weekly contracts carry 120,000 open interest and 45,000 volume — the highest liquidity concentration in the SPY weekly chain. Delta 0.52, gamma 0.012, IV 0.16.

## Recommendations

Put Wall Bounce is the preferred strategy while 580 holds as support. Consider scaling out of long calls near the 585 call wall.

# Daily Options Market Brief: iShares Russell 2000

**Date:** 2026-03-28  
**Domain:** Options Intelligence  
**Reference:** Underlying: iShares Russell 2000 (IWM), Regime: Post-FOMC Positive Gamma, KeyLevel: IWM Magnet 212

## Summary

IWM trades at 212.40 (prev 210.90) pinned to the 212 magnet level (strength 68.4, source DEX). Unlike SPX/SPY/QQQ, IWM still shows negative GEX (-165.2) at the 212 strike, reflecting asymmetric small-cap dealer positioning. NFP risk remains.

## Details

IWM 212P Weekly (OI 35,000, volume 9,500) is the most-traded hedge in the IWM chain. Delta -0.48, gamma 0.020, IV 0.22. Expect higher realized volatility vs. large caps.

## Recommendations

Negative Gamma Mean Reversion is the regime-aligned strategy for IWM. Use 212 as the pivot; fade stretches in either direction.

documents.title: Trade Journal Entry: SPX 5800C Scalp Long
documents.content:
# Trade Journal Entry: SPX 5800C Scalp Long

**Date:** 2026-03-28  
**Domain:** Options Intelligence  
**Reference:** Trade: SPX 5800C Scalp Long, OptionsContract: SPX 5800C 0DTE, Underlying: S&P 500 Index, Regime: Post-FOMC Positive Gamma

## Summary

Bought 5x SPX 5800C 0DTE at 24.80 with SPX trading 5820.50. Delta 0.55 long exposure with positive gamma regime tailwind. Closed at 25.48 for +$340 P&L.

## Details

Entry triggered by confirmation of Post-FOMC Positive Gamma regime (gex 245.6) and spot holding above the 5800 magnet. Put wall at 5700 defined max risk. 0DTE Gamma Scalp strategy (win rate 0.62) used.

## Lessons

Positive gamma compression kept realized vol low — next time, size up and hold longer. Theta was -12.50/day but was offset by favorable dealer hedging flow.

---

**Domain:** Options Intelligence  
**Reference:** Trade: SPY 580C Momentum Buy, OptionsContract: SPY 580C Weekly, Underlying: SPDR S&P 500 ETF, Regime: Post-FOMC Positive Gamma

## Summary

Bought 10x SPY 580C Weekly at 3.25 as SPY broke above 580. Exit at 3.43 = +$180 P&L. Put Wall Bounce strategy used off the 575 support defense into the 585 call wall.

## Details

SPY had been ranging between 580 support and 585 resistance. GEX at 580 = +280.1 confirmed dealer long-gamma hedging to compress moves into 585. IV 0.16, delta 0.52, gamma 0.012.

## Lessons

Respect call wall resistance — should have trimmed at 584 rather than chasing. SPY weekly OI of 120,000 provided tight bid-ask.

# Regime Change Analysis: Negative Gamma Regime

**Date:** 2026-03-20  
**Domain:** Options Intelligence  
**Reference:** Regime: Negative Gamma Regime, Underlying: S&P 500 Index

## Summary

Negative Gamma Regime detected at 2026-03-20T10:15:00 with gex -142.8 and flip level 5780. This was the earliest regime in the current cycle and preceded the Transition Regime.

## Details

Dealers were short gamma, amplifying directional moves in SPX. Flip level at 5780 was breached intraday, triggering the subsequent transition.

## Recommendations

Negative Gamma Mean Reversion was the appropriate playbook during this window.

# Regime Change Analysis: Transition Regime

**Date:** 2026-03-22  
**Domain:** Options Intelligence  
**Reference:** Regime: Transition Regime, Underlying: S&P 500 Index

## Summary

Transition Regime detected at 2026-03-22T14:30:00 with gex 12.3 (near zero) and flip level 5785. This regime was PRECEDED_BY Negative Gamma Regime and FLIPPED_TO Positive Gamma Regime.

## Details

Dealer gamma crossed from negative to positive. Choppy, indecisive tape typical of flip-zone action.

## Recommendations

Reduced position sizing and FOMC Straddle structures fit the transition window.

---

documents.title: Strategy Playbook: 0DTE Gamma Scalp
documents.content:
# Strategy Playbook: 0DTE Gamma Scalp

**Date:** 2026-03-28  
**Domain:** Options Intelligence  
**Reference:** Strategy: 0DTE Gamma Scalp, Regime: Positive Gamma Regime, Regime: Post-FOMC Positive Gamma

## Summary

0DTE Gamma Scalp (directional, win rate 0.62, avg return 8.5, max loss 500) is OPTIMAL_IN Positive Gamma and Post-FOMC Positive Gamma regimes. Trade example: SPX 5800C Scalp Long, QQQ 500C Gamma Scalp.

## Details

Entry: confirm positive net GEX at the ATM strike; buy the ATM call at bid-mid. Exit: scale out at +20% or when spot reaches next magnet.

## Risk Rules

Max 5-10 contracts on SPX, 8-10 on QQQ. Hard stop at -$500.

---

*Document generated for Options Intelligence context graph application.*

documents.title: Strategy Playbook: Negative Gamma Mean Reversion
documents.content:
# Strategy Playbook: Negative Gamma Mean Reversion

**Date:** 2026-03-28  
**Domain:** Options Intelligence  
**Reference:** Strategy: Negative Gamma Mean Reversion, Regime: Negative Gamma Regime

## Summary

Negative Gamma Mean Reversion (mean_reversion, win rate 0.48, avg return 15.2, max loss 800). OPTIMAL_IN Negative Gamma Regime. Trade example: IWM 212P Hedge.

## Details

Fade 2-sigma stretches at key levels. Size small given the lower win rate and wider max loss. Prefer names with persistent negative GEX like IWM.

## Risk Rules

Stop at -$800 per position. Do not average down.

---

*Document generated for Options Intelligence context graph application.*

documents.title: Strategy Playbook: Put Wall Bounce
documents.content:
# Strategy Playbook: Put Wall Bounce

**Date:** 2026-03-28  
**Domain:** Options Intelligence  
**Reference:** Strategy: Put Wall Bounce, Regime: Positive Gamma Regime

## Summary

Put Wall Bounce (momentum, win rate 0.71, avg return 6.8, max loss 350). OPTIMAL_IN Positive Gamma Regime. Trade example: SPY 580C Momentum Buy off SPY 575 put wall (prior session).

## Details

Buy calls when spot tests and holds a put wall in a positive gamma regime. Target the next magnet or call wall.

## Risk Rules

Stop if put wall breaks with volume. Max loss $350.

---

*Document generated for Options Intelligence context graph application.*

documents.title: Strategy Playbook: Pin Risk Butterfly
documents.content:
# Strategy Playbook: Pin Risk Butterfly

**Date:** 2026-03-28  
**Domain:** Options Intelligence  
**Reference:** Strategy: Pin Risk Butterfly, Regime: Neutral Regime

## Summary

Pin Risk Butterfly (neutral, win rate 0.38, avg return 45.0, max loss 175). OPTIMAL_IN Neutral Regime. Trade example: AAPL 230P Directional sized as a pin-risk structure around AAPL 228.50.

## Details

Buy ATM butterflies the day before expiration in neutral regimes with high magnet strength. Asymmetric risk-reward: low win rate but large avg return.

## Risk Rules

Max loss capped at debit paid; size so single loss stays under $175.

---

*Document generated for Options Intelligence context graph application.*

documents.title: Strategy Playbook: FOMC Straddle
documents.content:
# Strategy Playbook: FOMC Straddle

**Date:** 2026-03-28  
**Domain:** Options Intelligence  
**Reference:** Strategy: FOMC Straddle, Regime: Transition Regime, MarketEvent: FOMC Rate Decision Jan 2026

## Summary

FOMC Straddle (volatility, win rate 0.55, avg return 22.4, max loss 1200). OPTIMAL_IN Transition Regime ahead of FOMC events. Linked to FOMC Rate Decision Jan 2026 which CAUSED_FLIP to Post-FOMC Positive Gamma.

## Details

Buy ATM straddles on SPX 24-48 hours before FOMC. Close same-day post-announcement regardless of direction.

## Risk Rules

Max 3-5 straddles. Hard stop -$1200. Avoid overnight hold past event.

---

traces.task: Evaluate 0DTE trade entry on SPX given current dealer positioning and the Post-FOMC Positive Gamma regime

steps.thought: Check current GEX regime and dealer positioning for SPX
steps.action: Query the active Regime for S&P 500 Index and net gamma at the 5800 strike
steps.observation: SPX is IN_REGIME Post-FOMC Positive Gamma (gex 245.6). GEX @ 5800 SPX shows net_gamma 720.4 — dealers long gamma.

steps.thought: Identify key support and resistance levels from options positioning
steps.action: Find put walls and call walls for SPX via HAS_LEVEL
steps.observation: SPX has SPX Put Wall 5700 (strength 85.2) as support. Upside magnet at 5800.

steps.thought: Assess whether current price (5820.50) aligns with a 0DTE Gamma Scalp setup
steps.action: Compare spot to flip level 5770 and confirm Strategy OPTIMAL_IN current regime
steps.observation: Spot 5820.50 is above flip 5770. 0DTE Gamma Scalp is OPTIMAL_IN Post-FOMC Positive Gamma.

steps.thought: Calculate position size based on 500 max-loss budget
steps.action: Divide max loss by SPX 5800C 0DTE ask (25.20) * 100
steps.observation: 5 contracts at 24.80 = $12,400 debit, within tolerance; matches SPX 5800C Scalp Long trade pattern.

traces.outcome: Entry decision: Approve. Strategy: 0DTE Gamma Scalp. Contract: SPX 5800C 0DTE at 24.80. Quantity: 5. Max risk: $500.

traces.task: Respond to gamma regime flip from Neutral Regime to Post-FOMC Positive Gamma on SPX

steps.thought: Confirm the regime flip via FLIPPED_TO edge and timestamps
steps.action: Query FLIPPED_TO from Neutral Regime and check detected_at of Post-FOMC Positive Gamma
steps.observation: Neutral Regime (2026-03-27T11:00:00) FLIPPED_TO Post-FOMC Positive Gamma (2026-03-29T14:30:00). Order is consistent.

steps.thought: Assess open SPX positions affected by the regime change
steps.action: Find trades EXECUTES SPX 5800C 0DTE with open status
steps.observation: SPX 5800C Scalp Long (5 contracts) is active; delta 0.55 long, benefits from positive gamma.

steps.thought: Determine whether to hold, hedge, or add
steps.action: Check Strategy OPTIMAL_IN Post-FOMC Positive Gamma
steps.observation: 0DTE Gamma Scalp and Put Wall Bounce are both OPTIMAL_IN the new regime — hold and optionally add.

traces.outcome: Action: hold SPX 5800C Scalp Long; consider adding 1-2 more contracts. Positions adjusted: none. New exposure: +5 delta-adjusted SPX calls.

traces.task: Analyze breach of SPX Put Wall 5700 at $5700 on S&P 500 Index

steps.thought: Verify whether SPX spot approached the 5700 put wall
steps.action: Query Underlying S&P 500 Index spot_price and compare to KeyLevel SPX Put Wall 5700 price
steps.observation: SPX spot 5820.50 vs put wall 5700 — level is not breached, 120 points above.

steps.thought: Assess dealer hedging implications if spot moves toward 5700
steps.action: Check GEX @ 5800 SPX and modeled transition to 5700 via EXPOSURE_AT edge
steps.observation: GEX at 5800 is +720.4; a move toward 5700 would flip dealers short gamma and amplify downside.

steps.thought: Identify next support below 5700
steps.action: Query KeyLevel nodes for SPX with price below 5700
steps.observation: No deeper SPX levels present in the current graph — 5700 is the last put wall of record.

traces.outcome: Breach status: not breached (spot 5820.50, wall 5700). Next target on breach: below 5700 would expose dealers to negative gamma. Dealer flow: currently long gamma at 5800.

traces.task: Analyze 0DTE options expiration impact for SPX with SPX 5800C 0DTE contracts expiring 2026-03-28

steps.thought: Identify strikes with highest open interest expiring today
steps.action: Query OptionsContract nodes with expiry 2026-03-28 sorted by open_interest
steps.observation: SPX 5800C 0DTE (OI 45000, vol 12000) and QQQ 500C 0DTE (OI 85000, vol 32000) are the largest 0DTE positions.

steps.thought: Calculate gamma exposure removal from expiration
steps.action: Sum GEX at the 5800 SPX and 500 QQQ strikes
steps.observation: Expiration removes +720.4 GEX at SPX 5800 plus +360.3 at QQQ 500 — significant post-close repositioning expected.

steps.thought: Assess pinning probability at SPX 5800 max pain
steps.action: Compare SPX spot 5820.50 to the 5800 magnet strike
steps.observation: Distance is 20.5 points; positive gamma regime (gex 245.6) supports pin gravity toward 5800.

traces.outcome: Max pain: $5800 SPX. Pin probability: moderate-to-high given Post-FOMC Positive Gamma. GEX removal: -720.4 at SPX 5800 post-expiry.

traces.task: Position for upcoming FOMC Rate Decision Jan 2026 impact on S&P 500 Index

steps.observation: Flip not breached; regime holds. No hedge needed yet.

steps.observation: Invesco QQQ Trust IN_REGIME Post-FOMC Positive Gamma (gex 245.6).

steps.observation: GEX @ 500 QQQ has net_gamma 360.3 at strike 500; spot 503.20 is just above.

steps.observation: QQQ Gamma Flip 502 (strength 95.0). Above 502, dealers hedge mean reversion toward 500 magnet.

steps.observation: Entry 4.60 (mid of 4.50/4.70), stop at flip 502, target at 506 (next resistance).
